data.title: Regional bank stocks slide after two banks disclose bad loans
data.description:
The KBW Nasdaq Regional Banking Index (KRX) sank 5.7% in Thursday afternoon trading, its biggest drop since April, following the disclosures by two banks of irregularities related to some loans. 

After Wednesday's close, Zions Bancorporation (NASDAQ:ZION) said it found "apparent misrepresentations and contractual defaults" by the borrowers and supporting collateral when it reviewed two related loans made by its California Bank & Trust division. Zions took a provision for the full $60M outstanding under the loans.

On Thursday, Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) said it sued a borrower, alleging fraud in failing to provide collateral loans in first position, among other claims.

Zions (NASDAQ:ZION) stock plunged 12% and Western Alliance (NYSE:WAL) tumbled 10% in Thursday trading.

That apparently has investors wary of other regional banks. Among the biggest decliners in the sector: Flagstar Financial (NYSE:FLG) -6.4%, Banc of California (NYSE:BANC) -8.0%, Webster Financial (NYSE:WBS) -5.8%, Metropolitan Bank Holding (NYSE:MCB) -6.1%, Axos Financial (NYSE:AX) -6.6%, and Bank OZK (NASDAQ:OZK) -5.2%.

data.title: U.S. budget balance flips to $198B surplus in September
data.description: The U.S. federal government earned $198.0B more than it spent in September, reversing from the prior month's $345.0B deficit, according to the Treasury Department's monthly statement released on Thursday.

data.title: Pinnacle Financial outlines 13%-14% net interest income and 20%-22% non-interest income growth for 2025 as hiring momentum persists
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pinnacle Financial Partners (PNFP) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO M. Turner opened the call by highlighting, "this is one of the best in a long history of beat and raise quarters as has been our custom for a very long time." Turner emphasized ongoing momentum in hiring, asset quality, and market share gains, and specifically called attention to the company's track record of double-digit CAGR in loans and core deposits over nearly five years. He stated, "We expect balance sheet growth to continue based on the revenue producers that are currently on our payroll, but have not yet completed consolidating their books to us."

  • Turner stressed, "third quarter '25 is just another quarter on that march with third quarter linked-quarter annualized growth rates of 14.5% for noninterest-bearing deposits, 10.6% for core deposits, 8.9% for loans, 31.5% for revenue and 54% for adjusted EPS."

  • The CEO discussed the strategic significance of the merger with Synovus, explaining, "This merger is unique in its ability to run a differentiated service model, literally the best with a combined Net Promoter Score near 80." Turner underscored the importance of pre-merger decisions: "We finalized all the key leadership positions, having now pushed it down 3 levels into the organization."

  • CFO Harold Carpenter stated, "End-of-period loans increased 8.9% linked quarter annualized, but a little less than we anticipated, but still a strong effort by our relationship managers." Carpenter described ongoing hiring, robust loan pipelines, and a sustained outlook for loan and deposit growth. He announced, "Our outlook for the fourth quarter of 2025 is more bullish as our NIM should continue to increase with the anticipated 2 additional rate cuts."

  • Carpenter added, "We have increased our estimated growth range for net interest income and now believe our growth outlook will approximate a range of 13% to 14% over 2024 results." He also confirmed, "We are comfortable increasing our guidance for non-interest income from 12% to 15% growth to now 20% to 22% growth this year."

Outlook

  • Management increased end-of-period loan outlook to 9% to 10% growth for 2025. The estimated growth rate for total end-of-period deposits was raised to an 8% to 10% range for 2025.

  • The net interest income growth outlook was raised to 13% to 14% over 2024 results. The outlook for non-interest income was increased to 20% to 22% growth for 2025. Carpenter stated, "Given these matters, we and BHG are both comfortable in raising our earnings estimate for BHG earnings growth in 2025 to approximate 85% to 90% growth over the results reported in 2024."

  • The company anticipates a continued bullish NIM outlook in Q4 2025 and expects a stable effective tax rate in the low 18% range for the year.

Financial Results

  • Third quarter linked-quarter annualized growth rates included 14.5% for noninterest-bearing deposits, 10.6% for core deposits, 8.9% for loans, 31.5% for revenue, and 54% for adjusted EPS.

  • End-of-period deposit growth reached 6.4% linked quarter annualized. NIM increased 3 basis points to 3.26% in Q3.

  • Net charge-offs decreased to 18 basis points in Q3 from 20 basis points in Q2. The net charge-off outlook for the full year remains at 18 to 20 basis points. Provision to average loans is now projected at 26 to 27 basis points.

  • BHG generated over $40 million in fee revenues for the quarter, with expectations of approximately $30 million in Q4. BHG's on-balance sheet loan margins now exceed 11%. The company raised BHG's 2025 earnings growth target to 85%-90% over 2024 results.

  • Total expense outlook for 2025 was modified to a range of $1.15 billion to $1.155 billion.

Q&A

  • Jared David Shaw, Barclays: Asked about the pace of hiring and whether 300 relationship managers are available in the market. Turner responded that talent is available over time and reinforced the firm's unique recruitment model. Shaw also inquired about BHG and the impact of the pro forma balance sheet; CFO Carpenter said, "I think BHG's growth is going to be consistent going into next year."

  • Catherine Mealor, KBW: Sought clarity on BHG's growth sustainability into 2026 and fee income run rate. Carpenter replied, "No, it will grow. I think the quarterly run rates will be consistent going into next year... they're really excited about the way the production flows are coming in."

  • Anthony Elian, JPMorgan: Questioned hiring strategy post-merger. Turner explained, "I cannot imagine what would interrupt that cycle... the more people that we do hire, the more people that we can hire."

  • Stephen Scouten, Piper Sandler: Asked about market share opportunities and incremental risks. Turner stated, "I don't think there's anything there... everything we've encountered thus far is really on the positive side of the ledger."

  • Brett Rabatin, Hovde Group: Inquired about margin tailwinds and deposit growth dynamics. Carpenter cited deposit betas, noninterest-bearing account growth, and fixed-rate loan repricing as primary drivers.

  • Michael Rose, Raymond James: Asked about legacy vs. expansion market loan growth and CRE concentrations. Turner discussed the impact of seasoned bankers and hiring, and Carpenter confirmed the CRE book is granular and closely monitored.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on hiring, BHG growth, and merger synergies but generally maintained a constructive to slightly positive tone, seeking confirmation of sustainability and risk mitigation.

  • Management was highly confident in both prepared remarks and Q&A, repeatedly referencing momentum, discipline, and robust growth expectations. Phrases such as "I cannot imagine what would interrupt that cycle" and "our flywheel continues to spin" reflected strong conviction.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive and optimistic, particularly regarding the merger with Synovus, BHG's outlook, and the increased guidance ranges. Analysts were marginally more focused on sustainability and risk but did not express significant skepticism.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for net interest income growth was increased from 12%-13% to 13%-14%, and non-interest income growth was raised from 12%-15% to 20%-22% for 2025.

  • BHG's earnings growth estimate for 2025 was revised sharply upward from 40% to 85%-90% over 2024.

  • Management's confidence regarding merger execution and strategic hiring accelerated, with more detailed commentary on integration progress and leadership appointments.

  • Analysts in Q3 focused more on BHG's performance, merger synergies, and potential risks, whereas Q2 concerns centered around macroeconomic caution and deposit costs.

Risks and Concerns

  • Turner acknowledged integration work is "hard work," but stated, "I don't think there's anything there" regarding material negative developments from the merger process.

  • Management flagged the possibility of a temporary blip in CRE concentration against capital but expects a "quick earnback."

  • There is some caution regarding BHG's Q4 2025 income projection due to potential personnel costs and year-end factors.

  • Deposit and loan betas, as well as Fed rate decisions, remain areas to watch for margin and growth impacts.

Final Takeaway

Management emphasized that Q3 2025 delivered further evidence of Pinnacle Financial's ability to sustain double-digit growth in key financial metrics, supported by robust hiring, expanding market share, and strong fee income contributions from BHG. The company increased its guidance for both net interest and non-interest income growth for 2025, highlighted the strategic advantages of the Synovus merger, and reiterated confidence in maintaining its hiring momentum and differentiated service model as it enters a new phase as a larger regional bank.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Soleno Therapeutics gains amind renewed takeover speculation
data.description:
• Soleno Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SLNO) rose 1.8% amid a report on renewed takeover speculation. 
  • There's speculation that Soleno (NASDAQ:SLNO) is working with advisers from Guggenheim and Centerview after takeover interest, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert on Thursday. 
  • The latest report comes after Betaville in June reported that the biotech had received buyout interest from a Swiss-based pharma company and an American biopharma firm.

data.title: Thomasville Bancshares reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Thomasville Bancshares  press release (OTCPK:THVB): Q3  Net Income for the quarter of $11,717,444 compared to $9,386,870 for the same period last year, an increase of 24%.
  • YTD Net Income of $34,049,288 compared to $28,950,864 for the same period last year, an increase of 18%.
  • YTD Earnings per share were $5.33 (basic) and $5.13 (diluted).
  • YTD Return on Average Assets of 2.41% and Return on Average Tangible Equity of 24.73%.
  • Total Assets of $1.981 billion, an increase of $165 million over the same period in 2024.
  • Loans grew to $1.710 billion, an increase of $196 million or 13% year-over-year.
  • Deposits were $1.673 billion, an increase of $100 million or 6% year-over-year.
  • Regulatory Capital was $195 million or 10.06% of assets.

data.title: Nestlé rallies after guidance and strategy update
data.description:
Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY) (OTCPK:NSRGF) released its nine-month sales update on Thursday and reaffirmed its full-year 2025 guidance while unveiling a major cost transformation plan under new CEO Philipp Navratil.

The company maintained its full-year outlook, saying it expects organic sales to improve from 2024's rate of 2.2%. Management also anticipated margin stability, despite currency and inflation headwinds. Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY) plans to make continued investment in growth areas even amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

CEO Philipp Navratil said driving RIG-led growth is the company's number one priority. "We have been stepping up investment to achieve this, and the results are starting to come through. Now we must do more and move faster to accelerate our growth momentum," he added. The plan is to be rigorous in the approach to resource allocation, prioritizing the opportunities and businesses with the highest potential returns. "We will be bolder in investing at scale and driving innovation to deliver accelerated growth and value creation," Navratil highlighted. 

Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY) (OTCPK:NSRGF) said it would slash 12,000 white-collar jobs and an additional 4,000 manufacturing and supply-chain positions to reduce the global workforce by 6% over the next two years. Some white-collar roles will be automated as the reset.

Shares of Nestlé (OTCPK:NSRGY) rallied more than 9% in U.S. trading.

data.title: Westamerica GAAP EPS of $1.12, revenue of $63.99M
data.description:
• Westamerica press release (NASDAQ:WABC): Q3 GAAP EPS of $1.12.
  • Revenue of $63.99M.

data.title: Progyny gains as White House reportedly eyeing proposal to make IVF more accessible
data.description:
Shares of fertility benefits provider Progyny (NASDAQ:PGNY) gained on Thursday after The New York Times reported that the White House is set to announce a policy proposal to make in vitro fertilization more accessible.

Citing a White House official, the publication reported that the Trump administration is seeking to issue guidance on an initiative it identified as an employer benefit option that aims to enhance access to IVF and widen infertility coverage.

As part of the option, employers will be encouraged to provide their workers with IVF and broader infertility coverage directly, similar to their offerings for vision or dental coverage. Trump is expected to announce the initiative on Thursday afternoon from the Oval Office.

The White House expects more businesses to provide the benefit, as it is seeking employers to offer infertility coverage separately from major medical insurance plans. Soon after the announcement, the Labor Department will issue further guidelines to employers through its website, an official said.

IVF, a costly treatment option to address infertility, was a major theme on Trump’s campaign trail, and soon after taking office, the President signed an executive order to make the procedure more affordable in the U.S.

data.title: Western Alliance sues borrower, alleging fraud; stock tanks
data.description:
Western Alliance Bancorporation (NYSE:WAL) stock tumbled 10% in Thursday afternoon trading after the bank disclosed that it sued a borrower, alleging fraud related to collateral loans.

At the center of the issue is a note finance revolving credit facility to Cantor Group V LLC, secured by pledged commercial real estate loans and their cash proceeds, the company disclosed in a filing on Thursday.

In the lawsuit, Western Alliance (NYSE:WAL) alleges that the borrower failed to provide collateral loans in first position, among other claims.

"We have evaluated the existing collateral and believe it covers the obligation, based on 'as-is' appraisals," the company said in the filing. As a result, the company's total criticized assets are lower than they were on June 30, 2025.

Western Alliance (NYSE:WAL) confirmed its 2025 guidance and outlook as included in its Q2 earnings.

Last week, the bank had disclosed that it has some exposure to debt related to First Brands, a closely held auto-parts maker that filed for bankruptcy last month.

data.title: U.S. stocks turn red as investors assess trade war, corporate earnings amid government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages sank on Thursday amid increased volatility due to the ongoing U.S.-China trade conflict, while investors continued to assess earnings reports and the U.S. government shutdown entered its third week.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.5% in afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.3%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was -0.4%. 

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that China's lead trade negotiator Li Chenggang acted "unhinged" in Washington and warned that China would retaliate if the U.S. imposed port fees on its ships. Last week, U.S. President Donald Trump threatened to impose an additional 100% tariff on any goods coming from China in response to the new export controls on rare earth minerals.

Treasury Secretary Bessent had earlier said that President Donald Trump "is a go" for a meeting with Xi Jinping and that he was positive about the ongoing discussions with China. 

On earnings, Charles Schwab (SCHW) shares rose +0.5% after earnings beat. U.S. Bancorp (USB) also reported earnings growth, although shares were -0.5%.

“The good news is that earnings are doing the heavy lifting as we enter year 4 of this bull market. Following Friday’s decline, the P/E ratio is only up 1% year-over-year, while earnings are up 11%. This is in line with my expectation at the beginning of the year that after two years of substantial P/E expansion, earnings would become the dominant driver of returns in 2025,” said Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity.

“If history is any guide, we should see a meaningful bounce from the 7% growth estimate. A good bounce seems likely following the sharp uptick in earnings revisions in recent months,” Timmer added.

In other tech news, Salesforce (CRM) +4.8% and Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) announced an expansion of their existing partnership on Thursday to bring Gemini AI models to the former Agentforce 360 Platform.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 4 basis points to 3.99%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was 6 basis point lower at 3.45%.

The U.S. government shutdown is now entering its third week. Prediction market Kalshi now sees the government shutdown lasting more than 39 days.

The Fed balance sheet is due later in the day. 

U.S. stocks ended mixed after a choppy session on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gaining ground as upbeat earnings reports eclipsed U.S.-China trade tension worries.
